title: Best-case comparison count of insertion sort
statement_informal: Sorting a list of length n by insertion sort performs at least n-1 key comparisons (one per element after the first), achieved by an already-sorted input.

cost_model.notes: Each x <=? y test in the instrumented insert counts as one comparison. Inserting into a non-empty sorted prefix costs at least one; n-1 such inserts give the lower bound.

references: Lower-bound companion of insertion-sort-comparisons (worst case n(n-1)/2).
Software Foundations, Vol. 3: Verified Functional Algorithms (Appel).
